title: andropogon scoparius subvar. flexilis
intro: Andropogon scoparius subvar. flexilis, also known as broom grass or Indian grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its slender stems and feathery plumes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Andropogon scoparius subvar. flexilis have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to support overall health and well-being.
